steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The blank follows a complete independent clause ('The sand at Kaihalulu Beach.is a strikingly different shade') and precedes an explanation of why. The question asks which punctuation correctly joins them.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• "shade: though most": the colon introduces the explanation; 'though most sand.' opens a dependent clause that pairs with the main clause about iron oxide. ✓
  • "shade, though most": creates a comma splice between two full clauses. ✗
  • "shade, though, most": punctuates 'though' as starting a parenthetical fragment rather than a proper dependent clause. ✗
  • "shade, though. Most": ends the sentence mid-clause, leaving 'though' dangling with no clause to introduce. ✗
That matches the correct answer.
